content.text: An Azure Reservation is a one-year or three-year commitment to use a specific resource type at a specific capacity, in exchange for a discount of 30–65% compared to Pay-As-You-Go rates. Reservations are pre-paid either fully upfront or monthly; the discount is applied automatically to matching resources without any code change in the workload.

Reservations do not reserve capacity in a particular datacenter — they are billing instruments. They reduce the hourly meter charge for qualifying resources that match the reservation's scope, region, and SKU attributes.

attrs.alt: Azure Reservations discount comparison and scope options

content.text: Reservations are available for a wide range of Azure services. The most impactful are:

# List available VM reservations for a specific SKU and region
az reservations catalog show \
  --reserved-resource-type VirtualMachines \
  --location eastus \
  --query 'value[?contains(skuName, `Standard_D2s_v3`)].{sku:skuName,terms:terms}' \
  -o json

# Purchase a 1-year reservation for a D2s v3 VM (single subscription scope)
az reservations reservation-order purchase \
  --reservation-order-id $(python3 -c "import uuid; print(uuid.uuid4())") \
  --sku Standard_D2s_v3 \
  --location eastus \
  --reserved-resource-type VirtualMachines \
  --billing-scope "/subscriptions/$(az account show --query id -o tsv)" \
  --term P1Y \
  --billing-plan Upfront \
  --quantity 1 \
  --applied-scope-type Single \
  --applied-scope "/subscriptions/$(az account show --query id -o tsv)"

Most reservations are purchased through the Azure Portal under Cost Management + Billing > Reservations. The CLI purchase command requires the Reservation Purchaser role (built-in) on the billing account.

The scope determines which subscriptions receive the billing discount when a matching resource runs. Shared scope maximizes utilization when you have many subscriptions; single subscription scope isolates the discount to one team.
Matching resources in one RG only
All matching resources in one subscription
All subscriptions in the EA enrollment or MCA billing account
All subscriptions under the selected MG

# Change an existing reservation's scope to Shared
az reservations reservation update \
  --reservation-order-id <order-id> \
  --reservation-id <reservation-id> \
  --applied-scope-type Shared

# Change to single subscription scope
az reservations reservation update \
  --reservation-order-id <order-id> \
  --reservation-id <reservation-id> \
  --applied-scope-type Single \
  --applied-scope "/subscriptions/<target-subscription-id>"

# List all reservations in the billing account
az reservations reservation-order list \
  --query '[].{orderId:name,term:properties.term,state:properties.provisioningState}' \
  -o table

# List individual reservations within an order
az reservations reservation list \
  --reservation-order-id <order-id> \
  --query '[].{id:name,sku:sku.name,quantity:properties.quantity,util:properties.utilization.trend}' \
  -o table

# Get utilization summary for a reservation
az reservations reservation-summary list \
  --reservation-order-id <order-id> \
  --reservation-id <reservation-id> \
  --grain daily \
  --query '[-7:].{date:usageDate,util:avgUtilizationPercentage}' \
  -o table

A reservation sitting at 0% utilization still incurs the full committed cost. Monitor utilization weekly for the first month after purchase. If consistently below 60%, exchange the reservation for a better-matched SKU before the exchange window closes.

Azure allows reservation exchanges and refunds with some restrictions. You can exchange a reservation for a different SKU, region, or term at any time. Refunds (cancellations) are allowed up to a $50,000 USD cap per 12-month rolling window and are subject to a 12% early termination fee.

For VM reservations, Instance Size Flexibility (ISF) allows the discount to apply to VMs of different sizes within the same instance family (e.g., D2s v3 reservation covers D4s v3 at 50% rate). This is enabled by default for reservations not scoped to a resource group.

# Enable instance size flexibility on a reservation
az reservations reservation update \
  --reservation-order-id <order-id> \
  --reservation-id <reservation-id> \
  --instance-flexibility On

# Verify the setting
az reservations reservation show \
  --reservation-order-id <order-id> \
  --reservation-id <reservation-id> \
  --query 'properties.instanceFlexibility'

from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential
from azure.mgmt.consumption import ConsumptionManagementClient

credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
client = ConsumptionManagementClient(credential, subscription_id)

# List reservation summaries (daily grain for last 7 days)
summaries = client.reservations_summaries.list(
    scope=f'providers/Microsoft.Capacity/reservationorders/{order_id}/reservations/{reservation_id}',
    grain='daily',
    filter="properties/usageDate ge 2024-02-22 and properties/usageDate le 2024-03-01",
)

print(f'{'Date':<12} {'Used Hrs':>10} {'Reserved':>10} {'Util %':>8}')
print('-' * 45)
for s in summaries:
    util = s.avg_utilization_percentage or 0
    used = s.used_hours or 0
    reserved = s.reserved_hours or 0
    date = str(s.usage_date)[:10]
    flag = '  <-- LOW' if util < 60 else ''
    print(f'{date:<12} {used:>10.1f} {reserved:>10.1f} {util:>7.1f}%{flag}')

When purchasing a reservation, you choose between paying the full amount upfront or spreading the cost as equal monthly installments over the reservation term. The total committed cost is identical either way — the billing plan only affects cash flow.
Full term cost paid on purchase date
Maximizing cash discount; orgs with CapEx budgets
Remaining installments cancelled; past payments non-refundable
Orgs preferring OpEx; spreading commitment over time

For a 1-year reservation on a D2s v3 VM in East US (approx $66/month PAYG-reserved rate), the upfront cost is ~$795 vs ~$66/month on monthly billing — same total. Monthly billing reduces the refund-on-cancel risk since past payments are not refunded anyway.

SQL Database reservations apply to vCore-based compute tiers (General Purpose, Business Critical, Hyperscale) on both single databases and elastic pools. DTU-based tiers are not eligible for reservations.

# List available SQL Database reservation SKUs
az reservations catalog show \
  --reserved-resource-type SqlDatabases \
  --location eastus \
  --query 'value[:5].{sku:skuName,terms:terms}' \
  -o table

# Purchase a SQL DB reservation: GP 4 vCores, 1 year, monthly
az reservations reservation-order purchase \
  --reservation-order-id $(python3 -c "import uuid; print(uuid.uuid4())") \
  --sku GP_Gen5_4 \
  --location eastus \
  --reserved-resource-type SqlDatabases \
  --billing-scope "/subscriptions/$(az account show --query id -o tsv)" \
  --term P1Y \
  --billing-plan Monthly \
  --quantity 1 \
  --applied-scope-type Shared
SQL Database reservations do not cover storage (data + backup), I/O charges, or licensing costs (unless combined with Azure Hybrid Benefit). Only the compute component is discounted.

Cosmos DB reservations apply to provisioned throughput (RU/s) in Cosmos DB accounts. You purchase reserved RU/s in increments of 100 RU/s and the discount applies automatically across all databases in the scope.

# Purchase 1,000 RU/s reserved Cosmos DB throughput for 1 year
az reservations reservation-order purchase \
  --reservation-order-id $(python3 -c "import uuid; print(uuid.uuid4())") \
  --sku Cosmos_DB_100_RUs \
  --location eastus \
  --reserved-resource-type CosmosDb \
  --billing-scope "/subscriptions/$(az account show --query id -o tsv)" \
  --term P1Y \
  --billing-plan Upfront \
  --quantity 10 \
  --applied-scope-type Shared

# Verify Cosmos DB consumption against reservation
az costmanagement query \
  --scope "/subscriptions/$(az account show --query id -o tsv)" \
  --type AmortizedCost \
  --dataset-aggregation '{"totalCost":{"name":"Cost","function":"Sum"}}' \
  --dataset-filter '{"dimensions":{"name":"ServiceName","operator":"In","values":["Azure Cosmos DB"]}}' \
  --dataset-grouping '[{"name":"PricingModel","type":"Dimension"}]' \
  --timeframe MonthToDate \
  --query 'properties.rows' -o json

Azure provides reservation purchase recommendations based on your actual usage history. The Reservation Advisor (accessible in Cost Management) analyzes 7, 30, or 60 days of usage and suggests the optimal quantity and term to purchase.

# Get VM reservation recommendations based on 30-day usage
az consumption reservation recommendation list \
  --scope "/subscriptions/$(az account show --query id -o tsv)" \
  --look-back-period Last30Days \
  --reserved-resource-type VirtualMachines \
  --query '[].{
    sku:sku.name,
    region:location,
    qty:properties.recommendedQuantity,
    annualSavings:properties.totalCostWithReservedInstances,
    term:properties.term
  }' \
  -o table

# Get recommendations for SQL Database
az consumption reservation recommendation list \
  --scope "/subscriptions/$(az account show --query id -o tsv)" \
  --look-back-period Last30Days \
  --reserved-resource-type SqlDatabases \
  -o table

The recommendation engine uses a Look-back period of 7, 30, or 60 days. Use 30 days as a baseline — 7 days may miss weekly cycles, and 60 days may include usage patterns from before a recent architecture change.
Combining Reservations with Azure Hybrid Benefit

Azure Hybrid Benefit (AHB) applies Windows Server or SQL Server licenses to Azure VMs and SQL services, reducing the software component cost by up to 40%. AHB stacks with reservations: you get the reservation discount on compute AND the license discount on software, for combined savings of 60–75%.

# Apply Azure Hybrid Benefit to an existing Windows VM
az vm update \
  --resource-group myRG \
  --name myWinVM \
  --license-type Windows_Server

# Apply AHB to a SQL Managed Instance
az sql mi update \
  --resource-group myRG \
  --name mySQLMI \
  --license-type BasePrice

# Verify AHB is applied
az vm show \
  --resource-group myRG \
  --name myWinVM \
  --query 'licenseType'
Azure Savings Plans (introduced 2022) provide flexible discounts tied to a $/hour compute spend commitment rather than a specific SKU. Understanding when to use each helps maximize cost savings.
Applies to any compute in scope
Decision rule: prefer Reservations when you know the exact VM family and size will not change. Use Savings Plans when the fleet is heterogeneous or frequently resized.

# Purchase a Savings Plan (compute, 1-year, $5/hour commitment)
az savings-plan purchase \
  --billing-scope /subscriptions/<sub-id> \
  --display-name "compute-savings-plan-prod" \
  --term P1Y \
  --billing-plan Upfront \
  --commitment amount=5.0 currencyCode=USD \
  --applied-scope-type Shared \
  --sku-name Compute_Savings_Plan

# List active savings plans
az savings-plan list --output table
A reservation that sits underused wastes money. Azure tracks utilization percentage — 100% means the reserved hours are fully consumed. Target >= 85% utilization for healthy ROI.

# Get utilization summary (last 7 days) for all reservations in a billing account
az consumption reservation summary list \
  --billing-account-id <billing-account-id> \
  --grain Daily \
  --start-date 2024-06-01 \
  --end-date 2024-06-07 \
  --query "[].{Name:reservationOrderId, Date:usageDate, Util:avgUtilizationPercentage}" \
  --output table
Automate utilization checks via Azure Monitor Alert on the Reservation Utilization metric or schedule the Python script in an Azure Function to email low-utilization warnings.
Azure allows you to exchange reservations (swap to a different SKU within the same product family) or cancel for a pro-rated refund, subject to an annual $50,000 USD refund cap per billing account.
Open Cost Management + Billing, select Reservations.
Select the reservation to exchange and click Exchange.
Choose the replacement SKU, term, and quantity.
Azure creates a new reservation and prorates the remaining value of the old one as credit.
The exchange completes instantly with no downtime.

# CLI: split an existing reservation into two smaller ones
az reservations reservation split \
  --reservation-order-id <order-id> \
  --reservation-id <reservation-id> \
  --quantities 6 4

# Merge two reservations back into one
az reservations reservation merge \
  --reservation-order-id <order-id> \
  --sources '["<res-id-A>","<res-id-B>"]'
$50,000 USD per 12 months per billing account
Remaining term value minus 12% early termination fee
1-5 business days to billing account credit
Azure Advisor continuously analyses your VM usage and surfaces reservation purchase recommendations sized to your actual consumption patterns.

# List Advisor cost recommendations filtered to reservations
az advisor recommendation list \
  --category Cost \
  --query "[?contains(shortDescription.solution, 'reservation')]" \
  --output table

# For a specific resource group
az advisor recommendation list \
  --category Cost \
  --resource-group <rg-name> \
  --query "[?contains(shortDescription.solution, 'reservation')].{Impact:impact, Solution:shortDescription.solution}" \
  --output table

Advisor looks back 7 or 30 days (configurable) and accounts for existing reservations to avoid over-purchasing. Regularly reviewing Advisor recommendations ensures you capture new savings as workloads grow.

Azure Reservations provide 30–65% discounts on supported services (VMs, SQL, Cosmos DB, and more) in exchange for a 1- or 3-year commitment. The discount applies automatically via the billing system — no code changes required. Scope (single RG, single subscription, shared, or management group) controls where the discount lands. Instance Size Flexibility lets a VM reservation cover other sizes in the same family at proportional rates. Monitor utilization weekly after purchase and use the exchange window to correct SKU or region mismatches before costs compound. Start with PAYG to measure baseline consumption, then purchase reservations for the steady-state portion of your workload.
